page_content:
Limit on Special Courses

No more than 3 credits total of 269 or 289 (i.e. no more than one 200- level study- abroad course or one 200-level transfer course in a topic that is not fundamentally equivalent to one of our courses).

No more than 6 credits of 369 (i.e. no more than two 300-Ievel study

abroad courses in topics that are not fundamentally equivalent to our 300-Ievel courses).

No more than 6 credits of 389 (i.e. no more than two 300-Ievel special courses, as transfer credit or here on campus, on topics outside our regularly offered, "hard-numbered" curriculum)

GRADUATION PLANS Click here (https://www.arhu.umd.edu/academics/advising/academic- plans/) for roadmaps for graduation plans in the College of Arts and Humanities.

Additional information on developing a graduation plan can be found on the following pages:

http://4yearplans.umd.edu

the Student Academic Success-Degree Completion Policy (https:// academiccatalog.umd.edu/undergraduate/registration-academic- requirements-regulations/academic-advising/#success) section of this catalog
